The patient shows signs of possible primary ovarian insufficiency given her symptoms and hormonal profile; measuring serum estradiol will confirm low estrogen levels indicative of this condition.
Next steps should include assessing serum estradiol levels to diagnose any decline consistent with primary ovarian insufficiency.

To further investigate the cause, measure serum estradiol levels to assess for decreased estrogen production suggesting primary ovarian insufficiency.
\boxed{\text{Measure serum estradiol}}
``` Summary:

Elevated LH and FSH indicate possible primary ovarian insufficiency; measuring serum estradiol can confirm lower-than-normal estrogen levels.
\boxed{\text{Measure serum estradiol}}
